title: 基于 Rust 的 Vite 直接替代品发布，早期采用者报告构建速度提升 10 倍
author: Bruno Couriol
content:
Evan You, the creator of the Vue.JS front-end framework, recently announced a technical preview for rolldown-vite, a drop-in replacement for the Vite bundler written in Rust. Early adopters (e.g., Excalidraw, GitLab) report 3-16x faster builds and dramatically reduced memory usage.

While rolldown-vite is still a technical preview, You said it reached initial feature parity with today’s Vite, enabling companies and open-source projects to already test it with their codebase. Reported results show dramatically improved build speeds and reduced memory usage:

  • GitLab reduced build time from 2.5 minutes to just 40 seconds and cut their memory usage by 100x.
  • Excalidraw’s build dropped from 22.9 seconds to 1.4 seconds (16x faster).
  • PLAID Inc. saw one frontend’s build time fall from 1 minute 20 seconds to 5 seconds (16x faster).
  • Appwrite builds went from over 12 minutes to just 3 minutes, with memory usage being slashed by 4x.

Readers may review additional results on the dedicated GitHub page. Mercedes-Benz published an interesting write-up, emphasizing the engineering value of faster tools:

Given around 100 projects, an average of 600 commits per year per project, each one building and linting each time. We are talking about 500 hours saved per year, almost 21 days. Excluding the machine cost, since it is hard to calculate, we can imagine the engineering cost, both in productivity, focus, and just being able to enter the flow without hiccups.

Plugin and ecosystem compatibility require ongoing work. Plugin authors are encouraged to test and update their plugins with the help of the plugin author guide. While some Rollup plugins may work with minimal changes with rolldown-vite, others may have to perform structural adjustments that leverage the new Rust-powered internals.

You reported working on a full-bundle mode for the dev server, motivated by limitations encountered in Enterprise setups. The team also plans to extend the use of Rust to more of Vite’s internals in order to further deliver performance gains.

Developers drew a comparison on Reddit with Biome, another web toolchain that currently includes a formatter and a linter. Biome, however, currently has a smaller scope than VoidZero’s Oxc toolchain, which, besides Rolldown (bundler), already released a parser, linter, resolver, and transformer, with a minifier and formatter already prototyped.

One developer additionally noted amusingly on Reddit:

The saying used to be everything will be written in JS. But it seems that Rust has taken the place of that joke.

Rewrites of JavaScript/TypeScript toolchains in compile-to-native languages have been commonplace in recent years as JavaScript is dealing with larger codebases, including in enterprise environments. Microsoft has recently announced the porting of the TypeScript compiler to Go, offering a 10x performance improvement.

Developers are invited to check the Rolldown migration guide for the latest compatibility notes, known issues, and migration tips. For the latest updates and details, refer to the rolldown-vite changelog.
aiSummary: 一个基于 Rust 的 Vite 直接替代品 `rolldown-vite` 显示构建速度最高可提升 16 倍，同时显著减少内存消耗。
oneSentenceSummary: 一个基于 Rust 的 Vite 直接替代品 `rolldown-vite` 显示构建速度最高可提升 16 倍，同时显著减少内存消耗。
summary: 尤雨溪宣布了 `rolldown-vite` 的技术预览版，这是一个用 Rust 编写的 Vite 打包器替代品。像 GitLab 和 Excalidraw 这样的早期采用者报告称，构建时间（快 3 到 16 倍）和内存消耗（有人报告内存使用减少到原来的 1/100）都有显著改进。虽然仍在预览阶段，但它的目标是与当前的 Vite 实现初始功能对等，以便在现有代码库上进行测试。一个主要挑战是确保与广泛的 Vite 插件生态系统的兼容性，这需要插件作者进行调整。这一发展是用 Rust 重写 JavaScript 工具链以获得性能提升的日益增长趋势的一部分，与 Biome 和 TypeScript 编译器转向 Rust 等项目一致。未来计划包括一个完整打包模式，并扩展 Rust 在 Vite 内部机制中的作用。

mainPoints.point: `rolldown-vite`，一个基于 Rust 的 Vite 替代品，现已提供技术预览版。
mainPoints.explanation: 用 Rust 编写的 `rolldown-vite` 旨在替换 Vite 中的核心打包逻辑，以实现显著的性能改进。

mainPoints.point: 早期测试显示构建速度最高提升 16 倍，并大幅减少内存使用。
mainPoints.explanation: 像 GitLab 和 Excalidraw 这样的公司报告了显著改进，展示了大幅节省开发者时间与资源的潜力。

mainPoints.point: 确保插件兼容性是更广泛采用的关键挑战。
mainPoints.explanation: 虽然一些 Rollup/Vite 插件可能有效，但其中许多需要更新或进行结构性调整，才能充分利用新的 Rust 核心。

mainPoints.point: 这反映了为提升性能而用 Rust 重写 JavaScript 工具的趋势。
mainPoints.explanation: 这一举措与其他项目（如 Biome 和 TypeScript 编译器移植到 Rust）一致，旨在克服 JavaScript 执行限制。

keyQuotes: 早期采用者（例如 Excalidraw、GitLab）报告构建速度快 3-16 倍，并显著降低内存使用。
GitLab 将构建时间从 2.5 分钟缩短到仅 40 秒，并将内存使用量减少到原来的 1/100。
考虑到大约 100 个项目... 我们谈论的是每年节省 500 小时，将近 21 天。
插件和生态系统兼容性需要持续的工作。
过去常说一切都将用 JS 编写。但现在看来，Rust 已经取代了这个说法。
